title: A.2 — DSL Extractor: C++ MLIR Pass for TTIR Extraction + Bug Fixes
description: Replace fragile regex-based TTIR parsing with a robust C++ MLIR pass that walks the IR AST and emits structured JSON. Fix 3 bugs in grid_idioms.py and dsl_extractor.py. Implement comprehensive test suite with 10 reference kernel cases.

stories.title: Fix Bug 1 in grid_idioms.py: wrong parents depth
acceptanceCriteria: grid_idioms.py line 46: parents[3] changed to parents[2]
Comment updated to reflect correct path depth
Config loading test verifies get_capacities() loads from configs/ascend_910b3.json

stories.title: Create C++ ExtractTTIRInfoPass that emits JSON
acceptanceCriteria: Pass walks TTIR using MLIR AST API (no regex/text parsing)
Extracts persistent_loops from scf::ForOp with lb=program_id
Extracts tensor_ptr_shapes from triton::MakeTensorPtrOp result types
Detects has_dot from presence of triton::DotOp
Emits JSON to stdout via llvm::outs()
PassRegistration.cpp updated to register the pass

stories.title: Build and verify C++ pass on flash_attention.ttir
acceptanceCriteria: JSON output contains grid_axes: [0] for flash_attention.ttir
JSON output contains persistent_loops[0] with lb_is_pid=true, ub=4, step=20
JSON output contains tensor_ptr_shapes[0] = [32, 128] (tile size, not matrix bounds)
Direct subprocess invocation from Python succeeds and parses JSON

stories.title: Create Python mlir_parser.py wrapper
acceptanceCriteria: parse_ttir() function wraps C++ pass via subprocess
Returns dict with keys: grid_axes, persistent_loops, tensor_ptr_shapes, has_dot
Test verifies parse_ttir('test/flash_attention.ttir') returns expected dict

stories.title: Refactor dsl_extractor.py to use mlir_parser
acceptanceCriteria: Extract_from_ttir() rewritten to use parse_ttir() from mlir_parser
No regex functions remain in dsl_extractor.py (re.search, re.finditer, re.match)
Dispatch logic (persistent vs 1D vs 2D) preserved in structure
Uses structured info dict instead of regex results

stories.title: Fix Bug 3: Persistent kernel path uses correct work model
acceptanceCriteria: is_persistent branch in _extract_from_ttir() replaced with direct GridInfo construction
Uses round-robin tile assignment: program p gets tiles p, p+stride, p+2*stride, ...
Derives block_m from tensor_ptr_shapes[0][0] (32 for flash_attention)
work dict has 20 entries: work[0]=1, work[4]=0 for flash_attention

stories.title: Create test_mlir_parser.py with C++ pass output tests
acceptanceCriteria: Tests skip gracefully if build/bin/tritonsim-opt not found

stories.title: Create test_dsl_extractor.py with 10-kernel parametrized suite
acceptanceCriteria: REFERENCE_KERNELS list with 10 cases (K1-K10) from plan
K4 (Persistent flash_attn): occ=0.2, lb=1.0, work[0]=1, work[4]=0
Integration test verifies compute_grid_floor() returns t_grid_floor_us > 0

stories.title: Create test grid_idioms.py with idiom function tests
acceptanceCriteria: test_1d_exact: M=128, BM=32 → G=4, work[p]=32, load_balance=1.0
test_2d_tile_grid: M=128, N=256, BM=32, BN=64 → G=16, work[0]=2048
test_config_loading: temp JSON with ub.size_kb=192 → get_capacities()['ub']==192*1024

stories.title: Create fixture TTIR files for K4, K5, K10 test cases
acceptanceCriteria: test/persistent_21.ttir created: scf.for with ub=21, step=20, make_tensor_ptr with <tensor<32xf32>>
test/persistent_1.ttir created: scf.for with ub=1, step=20, make_tensor_ptr with <tensor<32xf32>>
Both files are valid minimal TTIR (can be parsed by triton-opt)
Files contain only structural ops needed for extraction (tt.get_program_id, scf.for, tt.make_tensor_ptr)

stories.title: Verify AC-1 through AC-7 acceptance criteria
acceptanceCriteria: AC-5: All 10 reference kernel cases pass
AC-6: pytest tests/ -q passes (tritonsim-opt tests skipped if not built)

stories.title: Document A.2 completion in a2_progress.md
acceptanceCriteria: Documents all 3 bugs fixed with file locations and line numbers
Documents all 10 reference kernel cases with expected values
Confirms ready for A.3 stage (Component Model Implementation)
